danger_signals.visual_form: Diverse enemy character sprites scattered throughout levels
danger_signals.meaning: Active threats requiring player engagement; different enemy types signal varying difficulty

danger_signals.visual_form: Larger, distinctive sprite at level endpoints; explosions upon defeat
danger_signals.meaning: Major threat requiring skill to overcome; visual reward (healing explosion) upon success

danger_signals.visual_form: Weapon degradation mechanic; visual reduction in attack power after player takes damage
danger_signals.meaning: Cumulative danger state; player becoming progressively weaker without healing intervention

danger_signals.visual_form: Vitality meter reduction; limited continues (five total)
danger_signals.meaning: Immediate danger of game-over; player vulnerability increasing

safety_signals.meaning: Resource availability; potential for weapon upgrades, potions, shuriken, grenades

safety_signals.visual_form: Explosion proximity-based health recovery upon boss destruction
safety_signals.meaning: Safe momentary state; reward for successful encounter completion
Active recovery mechanic; restoration of player health state

safety_signals.visual_form: Thunderstorm summoning when vitality exceeds half-meter
safety_signals.meaning: Powerful defensive/offensive capability available at high health; temporary advantage state

faction_indicators.environmental_markers: No faction-specific environmental markers; players are infiltrators in enemy territory

faction_indicators.symbol_or_sigil: Futuristic dictatorial stronghold; enemy soldier aesthetics
faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Multiple enemy types (soldiers, guards, specialized combatants) representing regime's military structure
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Well-guarded stronghold in futuristic New York City setting; fortified locations; industrial/urban environment

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Futuristic New York City setting; stronghold architecture; industrial enemy soldier designs
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy patrols and resistance throughout stages; boss encounters representing regime hierarchy

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Progressive stage movement toward stronghold center; increasing enemy density and difficulty
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Escalating enemy encounters; boss battles marking level completions; health carryover between levels indicating resource scarcity

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapon downgrade visual feedback after damage; reduced vitality meter display
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Decreased attack power; need to seek healing resources; tactical repositioning required

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Weapon upgrade levels (up to three increments); full vitality meter; special attack readiness
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Aggressive combat engagement capability; thunderstorm special attack availability at >50% health

environmental_storytelling.example: Five-level structure with fourteen stages progressively moving through Garuda's stronghold; environmental complexity increases toward final confrontation
Futuristic New York City backdrop with stronghold construction signals dystopian world-state and regime consolidation of power
Multiple enemy types throughout stages suggest military hierarchy and regime resource deployment; visual differentiation implies specialized roles
Health carryover between levels (not restored automatically) visually signals wartime/survival conditions; limited continues reinforce high-stakes mission context
Two-player simultaneous gameplay with Iga clan ninja (Hayate and Kaede) visually reinforces team-based infiltration narrative; shared continues emphasize collective mission outcome

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Access to thunderstorm special attack; enhanced combat capability; aggressive engagement possible
Weapon power reduced; vulnerability increased; tactical avoidance necessary; seeking healing items prioritized
Increased damage output; more effective range (kusarigama variant); tactical flexibility increased
Present throughout levels; attack on player proximity; varied difficulty by level progression
Explosion occurs; proximity-based health healing for player; signals level completion and temporary safety window

notes: Shadow of the Ninja communicates world-state primarily through environmental setting (futuristic dystopian NYC stronghold), enemy composition (regime military forces), and mechanical systems (health carryover, limited continues, weapon degradation). The game uses color differentiation for player characters (purple/orange ninja) versus regime forces. Danger is signaled through enemy presence, boss encounters, and mechanical vulnerability states. Safety is indicated through item availability and boss-defeat healing. The co-op structure with shared resources (continues, health carryover) reinforces narrative of a joint infiltration mission under resource constraints. Regional censorship differences (ninja references removed in EU version) indicate cultural communication boundaries external to game world-state. No explicit faction insignia or sigils documented; faction identity relies on role (ninja infiltrators vs. regime forces) and environmental context.
sources_used: tcrf: Shadow of the Ninja - The Cutting Room Floor (unused graphics, regional differences, title screen variants)
wikipedia: Shadow of the Ninja (gameplay mechanics, character descriptions, narrative context, setting details, weapon system)
